title: Why We Chose USC Over UChicago, CMU, Vanderbilt, UC Berkeley, Notre Dame, and more…
body: My daughter will be graduating from USC this May.  Originally she wanted to study on the east coast.  She got accepted into UMich (I know that is not east coast) but chose USC because she was afraid of the Asian hate crimes happening in the U.S..  We were unaware of the reputation ‘University of Spoiled Children’ nor were we aware of the location pretty much being Compton.  We are Asian from Hawaii and my wife wanted my daughter to experience studying in the U.S. mainland.  The USC experience has had its ups and downs for my daughter.  There were times she wish she applied to UCLA or other California universities but we had no experience or alumni history with mainland universities.
author: @miyakegaijin

title: A walking tour of Central Michigan University
body: I went to the first game of the football season against an fcs opponent the providence blue hose. We hung out with some of the coolest cmu tailgaters and although not being from the area we were welcomed with open arms. Going to the game and seeing all the faithful fans and students cheering for there chips was one of a kind. Later on that we visited Ann Arbor and caught a game AT&T he bug house but one thing i noticed was while it was still a cool experience it felt that you almost get lost in this sea of fans where at cmu it felt special to be cheering and more intimate with the fans and players. One of my favorite parts which is something ive never seen out of the 20+ division 1 football games ive been to was that after the game was over we were all allowed on the field. at a michigan/Michigan state game that would never happen. Overall the small town feel and the pride of the Chips made my trip to mt pleasant a very "pleasant" trip
author: @Quirkymacguffin
